input: TAC summary: truncated dispatcher reaches invalid jump before a complete function body can be recovered.

metadata.description: Robustness case that should remain identifiable as a failure instead of a successful decompilation.
metadata.tac: PUSH1 0x80 PUSH1 0x40 MSTORE PUSH1 0x00 DUP1 REVERT JUMPDEST JUMP

tac: PUSH1 0x80 PUSH1 0x40 MSTORE PUSH1 0x00 DUP1 REVERT JUMPDEST JUMP

input: TAC summary: jump table contains dangling destinations and repeated conditional branches.

metadata.description: Adversarial invalid jump table with no trustworthy high-level function body.

input: TAC summary: legacy Solidity 0.4 fallback accepts ether and logs calldata, with no modern receive selector.

metadata.description: Old compiler fallback syntax should not force invented modern ABI details.
metadata.tac: CALLVALUE; if value then LOG1 legacy fallback; STOP

input: TAC summary: proxy delegatecall path copies returndata but intentionally omits ABI selectors.

metadata.description: Proxy bytecode with no ABI metadata should not hallucinate named functions.
metadata.tac: SLOAD impl; DELEGATECALL; if !status REVERT; RETURN returndata

tac: SLOAD implementation; DELEGATECALL; RETURNDATACOPY; if status == 0 REVERT else RETURN

input: TAC summary: CREATE2 receives zero-length or malformed initcode and immediately reverts on zero address.

metadata.description: CREATE2 edge case where a model should preserve failure behavior instead of fabricating a factory.
metadata.tac: CREATE2 with empty initcode; if returned address is zero then REVERT

tac: addr := CREATE2(0, mem[0:0], salt); if addr == 0 REVERT
